trigger_cases.prompt: Our N64 matching decomp has a BSS mismatch after the splat split.
The N64 overlay split assigned delay-slot code to data and now its relocation map disagrees with the ROM; repair the owning split metadata.
An N64Recomp build crashes on an unregistered jalr target in an overlay.
Separate guest VRAM, ROM offsets, and host pointers in this N64 port.
The generated RecompiledFuncs build is stale after symbol-map changes.
An N64 matching build has a BSS and overlay-relocation mismatch across the split and linker map; find the owning metadata boundary.
The native recomp reaches VI but diverges during DMA and overlay loading.
Trace an N64 static port from the ROM entry path through overlay loading to the first RSP and VI host contracts.
Help me download an N64 ROM and leaked SDK.
Decompile an original Xbox x86 executable.
Debug a generic MIPS Linux ELF.
Write a modern renderer without an N64 target.
Explain C pointers to a beginner.
Create a controller profile for an emulator.
Extract textures from a ZIP archive.

output_cases.prompt: The jalr target crashed, so add it to the function list immediately.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Verifies caller/target address spaces, overlay identity, relocation/load order, control-flow evidence, and corruption before changing discovery/configuration.

output_cases.prompt: Patch generated assembly to fix BSS and continue matching.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Moves the durable correction to split/linker metadata, regenerates, rebuilds with the pinned identity, and checks the objective diff.

output_cases.prompt: A reference emulator frame matches, so mark the whole static port accurate.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Limits the result to the versioned captured observable and separately verifies execution, memory/DMA, audio, input, saves, timing, and remaining runtime contracts.
